ety: Zend varata, fr. var = S. वर् (वृणोति); cf. gard

Environs; vicinity. gird-navāh-i-shahr , n. m. The suburbs or neighbourhood of a city. gird h . 1. To follow ( pīchhe paṛnā , 1—3). 2. To hang upon; cling to one.
s. m. Round, circuit, circumference, environs.
subs: or ird-gird, adv. Around, about, all round, round about
s.m. A patrol; a watch; a superintendent (or inspector) of police or customs; — adj. & adv. All around, round

s.f. Bringing together, collecting; — surrounding, investing; siege; — going the rounds, watching, guarding, patrolling, looking after smugglers, &c.; inspecting; — division, beat; — gird-āwarī karnā, To go (one's) rounds, to patrol, &c.
vulg. gard-bād, s.f. A whirlwind, a devil or demon
s.m. A small round pillow (laid under the cheek); — a large cylindrical pillow

more: s. m. A loaf of a large and round form: barrel (of a drum), circle ( Harris ), orb.
subs: s.m. 'The disc of the heavens'; the sun (or the moon).

def: s.f. The wooden axis on which a well-pully (garrī) turns.
n. m. A support for a pitcher.

Farrukh. n. m. A chameleon. Lāl raṅg, murgā nahīṅ; sabz raṅg, nahīṅ mor; Lambī pūṅchh, bāṅdar nahīṅ; chār pāoṅ, nahīṅ ghoṛ . Riddle. Ans. Girgaṭ . It's red, but not a cock; it's green, a peacock not; No monkey though long-tailed; four feet, a horse it's not. girgaṭ ke se raṅg badalnā , v. n. 1. To change color (as the chameleon). 2. To turn red or pale with rage.
s.m. A species of round black gram smaller than a vetch; or, a kind of bean.
more: n. m. A kind of bean. (?)
s. A sort of round black grain smaller than a vetch, or a sort of bean.

def: s.f. Beseeching, imploring, humble entreaty.
adv. Beseechingly, entreatingly, with tears and humility.
v.t. To beseech, entreat, beg for with tears and abjectness, to implore earnestly and humbly.
s.m. Humble entreaty, &c. (see giṛgiṛāhaṭ).
s.f. = giṛgiṛāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Beseeching, imploring, humble entreaty.
s.m. The tree-lizard, a species of chameleon (commonly called by Europeans 'the blood-sucker,' on account of the redness of its throat)
more: s. m. 1. A lizard. 2. A chameleon.
subs: To change colour as a chameleon; (fig.) to turn red or pale (with rage, &c.).
ex: ارے ارے چلو، تم تو گرگٹ سے تیز رنگ بدلتے ہو
Are are calo tum to girgiṭ se tez rang badalte ho
Oh, come on! You change colors faster than a chameleon!

ety: gire, q.v.+bān, rt. Zend van, 'to protect, guard,' &c.; S. वन्
def: s.m. A collar; a cape; the opening or breast (of a garment); (fig.) the neck ( = girī); — a pocket
more: n. m. Z. grīūṅ . A collar; the breast (of a garment). girebān pakaṛnā, girebān meṅ hāth ḍālnā , v. a. To hold one by the collar; seize by the neck. girebān chāk k., girebān tār tār k. , v. a. To tear one's girebān to pieces. girebān meṅ muṅh ḍālnā , v. n. To look into one's own heart. Pīchhe auroṅ ko kuchh kahnā, pahle apne girebān meṅ muṅh ḍālo . Before another you presume to chide first turn your face towards your own heart.

s. m. 1. A collar (of a garment), cape. 2. Pocket, (fig.) the neck. گريبان گير girebān-gīr, adj. Seizing by the collar; an accuser, a prosecutor, plaintiff, complainant. گريبان مين منہہ ڐالنا girebān meṅ muṅh ḍālnā, To confess and be ashamed of oneʼs faults.
subs: (-kā), To lay hold of the collar (of); to seize by the neck
or girebān tār-tār karnā (-kā), To tear (one's) collar to pieces
adj. & s.m. Seizing by (or (one who seizes by) the collar; — an accuser, a prosecutor, plaintiff, complainant; an apologist
(apne), To look within oneself; to confess and be ashamed of (one's) faults

ety: perf. part. of giriftan, q.v.; and = Zend gerepta
def: part. Taken, received, accepted; clutched, seized; captured, made captive; — obstructed; shut, closed; involved (in), afflicted; embarrassed; — s.m. One who is made captive, &c., a captive, prisoner ( = giriftār)

subs: or girifta-ḵẖāt̤ir, adj. & s.m. Afflicted at heart; captivated; — one who is afflicted at heart, &c.
adj. & s.m. Seized by fate; at the point of death; — one who is seized by fate, &c.

ety: girift, q.v.+tār = Pehl. tar = Zend tar; S. तार्
def: adj. & s.m. Taken, seized, arrested, captured; involved (in), entangled; liable; stricken, smitten (with love, &c.), captivated; — one who is taken, &c.; a captive, a prisoner
more: adj. 1. Arrested; seized. 2. Involved, entangled (in a difficulty). 3. Captivated; smitten. Giriftār-i-ishq. giraftār k. , v. a. To apprehend; seize; capture. giraftār h. , v. n. 1. To become a prisoner. 2. To fall in love. ashkhās-i-giraftār-shuda . G. G. The persons arrested.
adj. Captive, prisoner, taken, seized, involved (in trouble, &c.), liable. گرفتار کرنا giriftār karnā, To captivate.
subs: v.t. To take, seize, arrest, apprehend, capture, to make a prisoner or captive of; to involve (a person, in, -meṅ); to captivate
(-meṅ), To be taken, &c.; to become a prisoner or captive; to be involved (in), to be smitten (with), to be enamoured (of).

def: s.f. Seizure, capture, arrest, apprehension; — captivity, imprisonment, bondage; embarrassment, entanglement.
more: n. f. 1. Seizure; arrest. 2. Captivity; imprisonment; bondage. 3. Embarrassment; entanglement. giraftārī kī darkhuāst k . G. G. To apply forarrest. giraftārī māl-i-mahsūlī , n. f. Seizure of smuggled goods. giraftārī-i-mufsidāṅ . Apprehension of rioters.
s. f. Bondage, captivity, imprisonment, embarrassment, seizure, capture.

ety: prob. akin to S. granthi, rt. granth or grath
def: s.f. A knot; knob; node; a joint, knuckle; an articulation; a division (one-sixteenth) of a gaz or yard, three finger-breadths; a purse; a gland; a kernel; (fig.) an entanglement, a difficulty; impediment (in speech); prejudice; misunderstanding, dissension (syn. gāṅṭh)
more: n. m. A planet. Grah apnā phal kar hī jāte haiṅ . Prov. The stars dont set till they have borne fruit. (Said of a conjunction of unlucky planets). grah ānā , v. n. To come under the influence of an evil star. grah-patr, grah-kunḍlī , n. A horoscope ( janampatr ). grah-dishā , n. f. 1. The influence of an evil star. 2. Evil days. grah dekhnā , v. n. To consult one's horoscope. nau grah , The nine planets. Kuchh na karegā to nau girah ke bāman to jimāëgā .

n. f. 1. See gānṭh , 1—4, 7, 9, 10. 1. Malāmat mere dil dene par itnī, Girah se terī, nāseh, kyā gayā hai? Sālik. Why these rebukes because I've given my heart away? What loss, O Moralist! have you incurred, I pray? 2. Jab tak the girah meṅ ahmaqoṅ ke paise Sab kahte the unko 'āp aise aise!' Muflis jo huë to phir kisī ne, aë Zauq, Pūchhā bhī nahīṅ 'voh kaun the aise taise? ' Zauq. When fools have cash all say 'Sir, you are so and so!' Poor grown none deign to ask 'who was this so and so?' 2. 1-16th of a gaz (yard); four finger breadths. girah bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To tie a knot in one's handkerchief. 2. To bear in mind; remember. girah-dār, gaṭhīlī , ( Bot .) Knotted; jointed. girah meṅ rakhnā , v. n. To keep; put in one's pocket.
s. m. A house, dwelling. ¶ grah ( ग्रह ) s. m. A planet.

s. f. 1. A knot. 2. Knuckle, joint, articulation. 3. A division of a gaz, three finger-breadths. گره پڙنا girih paṛnā, dissension arising between two people. گره کشا girih-kushā, Untier of a knot. گره کهلنا girih-khulnā, v. n. Restoration of friendship. گره گير girih-gīr, adj. Inclining to be knotty or curled.

ety: gir˚ = Prk. गिर(इ) = S. गिर(ति), rt. गॄ
def: v.n. To fall, drop, come down; to tumble; to alight, to perch (on, -par), to descend; to fall (upon, -par), to attack; to swoop down (upon); to hang (upon), to pursue; to befall, betide (as āfat, or 'calamity'); to fall (in price), to abate; to sink, to be allayed, to lull (as the wind); to fall (from, -se, the esteem or regard of), to be reduced, be degraded; to be brought low, to be ruined; to be beaten, be overthrown; to fall (in battle); to drop down dead; to drop (from the mouth), to be spit out; to be affected (with); to be taken (with); to fall through, miscarry, fail
more: v. n. S. गल See پرَنا 1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 15. 1. To fall; drop; come down. 1. Bhūke ko bhūke ne mārā, yeh bhī girā, voh bhī girā . Prov. One starveling dealt another one a blow, this one fell down and that one fell also. 2. Ḍhāte the vāṅ mazūr to tan kī mahalsarā, Yeh ghar banā rahe the divāleṅ uṭhā uṭhā, Is meṅ qazā kā rāj jo koṭhe pe ā chaṛhā, Shahtīr sā woh qad thā so kham ho-ke jhuk gayā, Girne lagī kaṛī pe kaṛī tab khabar paṛī . Nazīr. There men on pulling down this earthy frame intent, Here raising walls and building chambers diligent, Just then Death's mason mounted on the tenement, The rigid beams were bent, their fall was imminent, And when they toppling came down then all were attent. 3. def: v. n. To fall down.

s.m. A god, a deity.
v.t. To cause to be dropped, or to be thrown down; to cause to fell; to have (one) overthrown; to cause to upset, &c. (cf. girānā).

v. a. Caus. of گارَنا q. v.

The holder of a pledge or mortgage. girvī rakhnā, gahne rakhnā , v. a. To pawn or pledge. girvī rakhne-vālā , n. m. 1. A mortgager. 2. s.m. Crying, weeping, lamentation, plaint

ety: imperf. part. of girīstan; rt. Zend gared (garedh), fr. gar = S. गर् (गृणाति)

Songstresses call together, dear! Joy filling songs I fain would hear! byāh yā shādī kā gīt , A marriage song. debī ke gīt , Hymns to a goddess.
s. m. (dakh. s. f.) A song, singing. گيت گانا gīt gānā, To sing.
subs: To sing a song or hymn; to sing; — to be piping one note, to be ever harping upon one strain; — to tell a long story
A song-book, a hymn-book; the Book of Psalms.

def: n. f. Talking English, so called by Natives unacquainted with the language. Giṭ piṭ, giṭ piṭ gorā bole, laiṭ paiṭ Kaptān, Kurtī pahn tilaṅgvā kūde, Mejar kare kamān . The soldiers talk ' giṭ piṭ ,' the Captain 'left, right' bellows, and The Sepoys prance in uniform, the Major has command.
s.f. A song; a sacred song or poem, a rhapsody or episode (as the Bhāgavat-gītā, an episode of the Mahābhārat)
s.m. A sort of coarse woollen cloth; a coarse kind of carpet not coloured.
more: s. m. A coarse kind of carpet not coloured.

s.f. A flat round pebble; a shard (such as is used in playing at ducks and drakes).

s.f. The game of ducks and drakes (syn. ćhićhlī).

s.f. = giyāh, q.v. s.f. Grass; straw; green herbage (syn. kāh).

more: s. m. Benefitting, providing goods for sale or use, a badge to distinguish soldiers or sectaries.

ety: v.n. fr. اغاث ag̠ās̤a, 'he aided or succoured,' iv of غوث; see g̠aus̤
def: s.f. Aid, succour, deliverance from difficulty or distress; redress, listening to complaints.
more: (from غوث) s. f. Listening to complaints, redressing wrongs, assistance, redress, succours.

My drink my heart's blood, and my liver is my food— Such nutriment held for my soul's distracted good. gizāë latīf, halkā khānā . Light food or diet. gizā yā hāzimah ke mutaalliq , ( Med .) Alimentary.
s. f. Aliment, diet, provision, food. (Plur. اغذيه ag̠ẖẕiya ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غذا (ġizā), from Arabic غِذَاء (ḡiḏāʔ).

def: s. f. Vanity, vain, rash, absurd talk or act.

def: s.f. Diet, aliment, &c.

more: n. f. S. ग्लानि. 1. Moisture ( tarī , 1, 4, 5). 2. Aversion; disgust.

def: s.f. Weariness, languor, lassitude, fatigue, exhaustion; inertness; depression (of mind); debility; sickness; — unwillingness, reluctance; aversion, disgust.

Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)

Conj. Notwithstanding that; though; although; even if; however.
adj. ( In Comp . only) A speaker, teller, as bad-go evil-speaker. goind'ah , n. m. An informer; a spy.
s. m. f. A cow, one of the cow kind.

s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow

s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds

or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

(gosh = gos, gen. of go), s.m. A cow's foot or hoof; — mark or impression of a cow's foot; — puddle or water sufficient to fill a cow's foot-mark; a small puddle; — a measure of capacity (as much as a cow's foot-print would hold)
vulg. goshṭh, s.m. An abode for cattle; cow-house, cow-pen; — any place where animals are kept; — a station of cowherds
s.f. An assembly, a meeting; a society, an association; a confederacy; — family connexions (esp. the dependant or junior branches); — conversation, discourse, dialogue; dispute

s.m. A cow's ear; — a species of deer, Antilope picta (syn. nīl-gāʼo); — a mule; — a kind of serpent; — a span (from the tip of the thumb to that of the ring-finger); — a place of pilgrimage on the Malabar coast (sacred to Śiva); Śiva (as there worshipped)
(for go-karṇī), s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides, and its flower

s.m. A herd of kine; a multitude of cattle; a cow-station, cow-house; — a village or tract of the Jamna (the country around Vr̤indāvan, the residence of Krishṇ during his youth)
s.m. A measure of distance (being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard)

or gau-mukh, s.m. A kind of musical instrument (a sort of horn or trumpet); — a hole in a wall (of a peculiar shape), made by thieves, &c.; a breach (syn. seṅdh)

or gau-mutr, s.m. Cow's urine (which has to be imbibed in certain kinds of penance)

or go-medak, s.m. A gem or precious stone brought from the Himālaya and the Indus (described as of four sorts, — white, pale yellow, red, and dark blue); a kind of yellowish or tawny gem, a topaz; — go-meda-sannibha, s.m. Chalcedony or opal
s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow
s.m. (lit. 'having a nose like that of an ox'), A large kind of serpent, a boa-constrictor

s.m. lit. s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

def: s.f. (dialec.), A tick, a cattle-louse.

subs: adj. f. (m. -bharā), Having a child in arms or in the lap; — with child
or god phailānā (apnī), To spread out the skirt or mantle (to receive anything); to ask for, to beg
(-ko), To give one's own child to another to adopt
or god-kā baćća, s.m. A child in arms
(-ko), To carry (a child) in the arms; to nurse (or bring up); to be the mother of a child

def: s.m. The brain (see gūdā).

n. f. S. क्रोड 1. (Contemp. gudakṛā ) The lap. Terī god bharī rahe! Benediction. May your lap be ever full (of children)! 2. Adoption of a child. 3. A cocoanut with some muṭhiā (a sweetmeat) and a nāle kī joṛī (skein of red thread) presented to a bride at different ceremonies. god bharnā , v. a. To give cocoanut, etc. to a bride. god-bharī ; Bhoj. khoṅīchhā bharal , adj. Blessed with a child in arms; with child. god pasārnā, yā phailānā , v. n. To spread out one's mantle to receive anything; hence, to ask; beg. god pasār-ke kosnā , Wom., v. n. To call down curses upon. god denā , v. a. To give one's child to another to adopt. god kā bachcha, god kā , n. m. A child in arms. God kā ḍāl-ke peṭ kī ās . Prov. The child in her lap deceased, on her womb she relies for another. god khilānā , v. a. 1. To carry in one's arms (a child). Jo jag janm dīye Jadu nandan, so sukh sampatī de, Nand lālā, Khāne ko bhaṅg, nahāne ko Gaṅg, chaṛhe ko turaṅg, oṛhe ko dushālā! Pān purān de, chhīr de khāne ko, ati sundar god khilāne ko bālā! Wom. O son of Nand who mad'st the world, send wealth and ease! Give bhang to chew, Ganges to bathe in, a horse to ride, a shawl to wear! Old pān, and milk, and a handsome child in the lap to feed! 2. To be the mother of a child. 3. To nurse or bring up a child. Tum to, chhailā, merī god ke khilāë! Mo se jin kaho aisī batiyāṅ! Wom. song. I've fed you in my lap, you reprobate! Say not to me such words indelicate! god lenā , v. a. 1. ( god meṅ lenā ) To take in the lap. 2. To adopt one ( mutabannā k .). god meṅ , adv. In one's lap. god meṅ ā baiṭhnā , v. n. To leap into one's arms.
s.f. An uncommon form of god, 'lap,' q.v.
s.m. A godown, warehouse; a store, heap; the stern of a boat (because stores, &c. are kept there).
more: n. m. Cor. of godown. 1. A godown; warehouse. 2. A heap; store.

ety: god˚ = Prk. गोद्द(इ), or गोद्दे(इ), fr. S. गोर्द; — or prob. another form of goḍnā or goṛnā = koṛnā
def: v.t. To prick, puncture, goad; to dot, mark (the skin) with dots; to tattoo; — to scratch; to dig, turn up (the soil); to hoe; — to wound or lacerate the feelings of (a person); — to sharpen the edge of (an instrument, &c., — as the roller of a cotton-gin)
more: Mār., v. a. 1. To prick; puncture; goad. 2. To tattoo. 3. To hoe; dig; turn up. 4. To wound one's feelings. 5. To sharpen the edge of the roller of a cotton gin. godnā , n. m. 1. Marks of tattooing. 2. A kind of hoe used for digging up sugarcane and the like.

Rus. To have sexual intercourse with. Oh re rāj! terī be be ne goḍāṅ! bābā jī ke maṭh kī gumṭī ākās māṅ lā dī! Rus. Ho, mason! may your sister be—if you have n't made our reverend father's tower touch the sky!
v. a. To prick, to puncture, to dot, to mark with dots (the skin). s. m. Marks of tattooing.
subs: s.m. Mark of tattooing; — a kind of hoe (used for digging up sugar-cane and the like).

s. m. 1. A lizard, iguana. 2. A gangetick alligator. ¶ gūh ( गूथ ) s. f. Human excrement, ordure, dung.

n. f. Treading out grain by bullocks.
s.m. (dialec.) Cow-house, cattle-shed.
s.m. (rustic) The inclined path along which the bullocks move in drawing water from a well.
n. A ramp along which bullocks draw water from a well.

ety: S. गो+धर or धार, rt. धृ; see dharnā
def: s.m. A broad pathway for cattle.
n. 1. A house. (?) 2. A summons to arms, similar to the fiery cross. 3. A pathway for cattle.
more: n. m. 1. Nature. 2. Descent; extraction. 3. A pearl (جوﮩر 1).

def: s.m. A call or summons to arms (similar to the fiery cross).

ety: S. गो+सारः, or ˚har = dhar˚; see dharnā
def: s.m. Cow-dung ( = gobar); dried cow-dung ( = uplā).
s.m. Cry, call, outcry; shout; halloo; tumult, uproar, hubbub, din; alarm; — fray, fight; — abuse, obscene language.

n. m. 1. A call or cry for help. Hauṅ aprādhī janam ko nakh-sikh bharā bikār, Tum dātā, dukh-bhanj ho, morī suno, Nāth gohār! A sinner from my birth, in sin from head to foot I lie, O generous Giver! Comforter! but listen to my cry! 2. Tumult (اولا 2). 3. Abuse; obscene language. 4. Fighting with clubs or sticks. guhār laṛnā , v. a. To abuse one another.
more: n. f. An unruly mob; rioters.

s.m. (f. -ī), One who is cried to for help; one who comes to the rescue; — an ally, assistant, helper, succourer.
more: s. m. An ally, succourer, an assistant.

def: v. a. To bawl; call. Runḍ parchaṅḍ munḍ binu dhāvahiṅ, 'Dharu dharu, māru māru' gohṛavahīṅ . Rāmāyan. Their headless trunks all swiftly fly, 'Seize, seize, strike, strike,' the hue and cry.
v.n. & t. = gohārnā, q.v. v.n. (dialec.) To cry out, to call (to), to bawl, shout, halloo; — v.t. To call to, shout to, &c.
s.m. = gohūnā, q.v. s.m. A large serpent destructive to cattle; a boa-constrictor.

s.m. The plant Ruellia longifolia; the fruit of this plant; — the plant Tribulus lanciginosus; the thorny seed of this plant, caltrop; — an ear-ring shaped like this seed; an anklet of tinkling ornaments (of the shape of the gokhrū seed); — thorns which get in between the hoofs of cattle; — iron crowsfeet thrown on the ground to check an advance of cavalry; — a joint; — a hard corn (on the foot); — the tragus (of the ear); — narrow twisted lace; an ornament of raised or fretted lace of gold thread (used to throw out in relief the flowers on brocade, &c.).
more: n. m. S. गोज्ञुर cow's hoof. 1. A creeper, the fruit of which is used medicinally. 2. The thorns which get in between the hoofs of cattle. 3. An iron crows foot thrown on the ground to check an advance of cavalry. From this plant seems derived the name of the same instrument among warlike nations, as the khasak of the Persians, the tribulus of the Romans, the chausse-trape of the French, English caltrop , Spanish abrojo. Elliot . 4. Corns. 5. A tragus of the ear. 6. An ear-ring shaped like the fruit. 7. Narrow twisted lace. An ornament of raised or fretted lace wrought of strong gold thread, and serving to relieve or damask the flowers on brocade, etc.
s. m. 1. Name of an ornament (bells tied round the ankles). 2. A joint. 3. Name of a plant. (Tribulus lanuginosus, or Ruellia longifolia: Roxb. ). بڙا گوکهرو baṛā gokhrū, Pedalium murex.

more: n. m. P. گوله 1. A large ball. 2. A cannon-ball; a bomb (firework). 3. The ordeal of carrying a red hot cannon ball, formerly much used in fixing boundaries. 4. A cocoanut or shell. 5. The kerb or curb of a well. 6. A pier. 7. A round beam. 8. ( Arch .) The astragal. 9. A herd of cattle. 10. A mart, esp. for grain. 11. A low-flying swift pigeon, opp. of nisāorā . 12. A swelling. 13. Colic. golā-dan , adj. Round and fat (a person). golā-dhār barasnā , v. n. To rain in torrents. golā sohan , ( Mech .) A round file. golā mārnā , v. n. n. m. 1. A village (گانو). 2. A country road; cart-road. Dhagṛoṅ se tū phire lipaṭtī galiyāre aur goṅḍe māṅ, Buḍḍhā, bālā, koī na chhoḍā, khāk paṛe tere chauṅḍe māṅ! Play. You wander in the streets embracing all the town, Nor old nor young you spare, down to the dust your crown! 3. A yard. 4. (Hin) Alms given to beggars on the arrival of the marriage procession at the bride's village. gauṅḍā sejnā , v. a. To distribute alms to beggars at a marriage. gauṅḍe-vāliyāṅ , n. f. Country women collected at a marriage.
s. m. A thin dough or pap made of the flour of Cicer arietinum, for feeding birds.

subs: The leg to break or to be broken; — the legs to pain, or to give way (through fatigue, &c.); — (fig.) to have all spirit taken out of one; to be disappointed or discouraged
or gor paṛnā (-ke), To touch the feet (of a person) reverentially; to fall at the feet of.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of fair complexion, fair, white; — red (when applied to cattle); — an epithet of Śiva; — a European soldier, a private; — a European (who is not a gentleman)
more: adj. 1. Fair; white. Kuchh nahīṅ kām hai liyāqat kā, Martabah hai to gorī rangat kā . No need here of ability, for fair skins sole all dignity. Oudh Punch. 2. Beautiful; handsome. gorā , n. m. 1. A European soldier; a private. 2. A European who is not a gentleman. gor'ī , Poet. n. f. A beauty; a belle. Gorī ke nainā aise jaise barad ke sīṅg . Rus. My mistress' eyes as sharp as bullock's horns.

n. m. S. कुपर 1. The leg. 2. The knee; knee pan. goṛe ṭūṭnā , v. n. 1. To be broken (the leg). 2. To be too tired to walk. 3. To be disappointed, discouraged. goṛe paṛnā, yā lagnā , Wom., v. n. To touch one's feet reverentially. Goṛ lāge, māhrāj! goṛe pasārnā , v. n. 1. To stretch one's legs. 2. To die; expire. 3. To lead an idle life.
adj. Fair (complexioned). ¶ गौरा gaurā, s. f. A name of the goddess Pārvatī: see گوري gaurī.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑁄𑀭 (gora), from Sanskrit गौर (gaurá).

def: s.m. The leg, foot ( = goṛ); — a cord or rope for tying the legs of an animal; a fetter; tether
subs: or goṛe lagnā (-ke) = goṛ lagnā, &c., q.v.s.v. goṛ
(apne), To stretch out the legs; — to die; — to lead an idle life.

def: adj. (dakh.) Red and white.
s.m. A corr. form of gorakh-dhandhā.
s. m. (dakh.) 1. Living, life: see زندگاني zinda-gānī. 2. Distraction, alarm, disquietude: see تشويش tashwīsh.
s.m.(?) Name of a clan of Rājpūts in the Agra district.
s.m. = goṛait, q.v.
more: n. m. A village watchman or informer.

ety: or gorait; prob. S. घोर (rt. घुर्)+ait = Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्; — cf. gohrānā or gohārna
def: s.m. A village watchman (the watchman being in the habit of bawling aloud at night); an intelligencer.

s.m. = gorakh-dhandhā, q.v. s.m. Living, way of living; — a troublesome or trying life or existence, distraction, disquietude; — an intricate or difficult affair or problem; a puzzle; a labyrinth or maze; — a Chinese puzzle (consisting of a number of pins passed through holes in an oblong plate; the pins having rings at one end through which an oblong ring is passed); — an intricate kind of lock, a puzzle-lock (Pers. syn. qufl-ě-waswās); — a kind of child's toy, having a string attached to it, which, when extended in one direction turns out to be of one colour; and when extended in another direction, turns out to be of another colour; — any complicated or puzzling plaything, or machine, &c.
more: n. m. 1. An intricate or difficult affair or problem. 2. A labyrinth; maze. 3. A Chinese puzzle; a puzzle lock, etc., a complicated machine.

def: s.m. = gorakh-dhandhā, q.v. s.m. Living, way of living; — a troublesome or trying life or existence, distraction, disquietude; — an intricate or difficult affair or problem; a puzzle; a labyrinth or maze; — a Chinese puzzle (consisting of a number of pins passed through holes in an oblong plate; the pins having rings at one end through which an oblong ring is passed); — an intricate kind of lock, a puzzle-lock (Pers. syn. qufl-ě-waswās); — a kind of child's toy, having a string attached to it, which, when extended in one direction turns out to be of one colour; and when extended in another direction, turns out to be of another colour; — any complicated or puzzling plaything, or machine, &c.

ety: gorakh, prob. a proper name = S. गो+रक्षः+dhandhā, q.v.
def: s.m. Living, way of living; — a troublesome or trying life or existence, distraction, disquietude; — an intricate or difficult affair or problem; a puzzle; a labyrinth or maze; — a Chinese puzzle (consisting of a number of pins passed through holes in an oblong plate; the pins having rings at one end through which an oblong ring is passed); — an intricate kind of lock, a puzzle-lock (Pers. syn. qufl-ě-waswās); — a kind of child's toy, having a string attached to it, which, when extended in one direction turns out to be of one colour; and when extended in another direction, turns out to be of another colour; — any complicated or puzzling plaything, or machine, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) Destroyer of a religious faith.

ety: gorā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Fairness (of complexion); whiteness (of colour) mixed with red; ruddiness.
s. (dakh.) Partridge.

n. m. Z. gavars . Milk, curds, etc. goras'ā ; Bhoj. gorsāh , n. A child brought up on cow's milk. gauṛ'hī , n. m. Pasturage. gaū-sālā, gavārī , n. m. A cow-shed. gaū-kos , n. m. A land measure, being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard.
more: s. m. Milk, butter-milk, curdled milk.

def: s.m. See s.v. go. s.m.f. Water; — s.m. Name of a Rishi; — the sacrifice of a cow; — the thunder-bolt; — the sky; heaven, paradise; — the sun; the moon; a member (of the body); — hair (of the body); — s.f. Speech; Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech; — the earth, the world; a mother; — a quarter of the horizon (as east, west, &c.); — the eye; — an arrow; — adj. Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)
more: 1. adj. (A child) reared with the milk of cattle. 2. s. m. A sodomite.
subs: vulg. gavāksh, gawāććh (˚va+ak˚), s.m. 'A bull's-eye'; an air-hole; a loop-hole, a skylight; a window; lattice; — the light admitted by a window
s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow
s.m. (f. -ī), Cow-keeper, cowherd; — ʻa protector of the earth,' a king; — the cowherd Kr̤ishṇa (one of the epithets of Kr̤ishṇ); — a proper name (of men)
s.m. Title of an Upanishad in honour of Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Having the charge of kine; — protecting the earth; — a cowherd; — a king
s.m. 'The lord of a herd of cows,' a bull
s.m. 'Son of a cow;' a simpleton; a fool
vulg. go-pasu, s.m. A sacrificial cow
vulg. go-pasutā, s.f. The condition of a cow about to be immolated
or gau-thān, s.m. The place where the cattle of a village are assembled; a station for cattle; a cow-pen ( = go-sthān)

adj. & s.m. Ranged or grazed over by cattle; — offering range (or field or scope) for action; within the range (of), accessible, attainable; within the power (of); within sight (of), visible, perceptible; perceived, known; — the range of cattle, pasturage, pasture-ground; range or reach (of the eye, or the understanding, &c.); — the range (or the conjunction or influence) of the planets; — an object of sense (as sound, shape, &c.); — an object of devotion; — perception; information acquired by the perception of the senses
or gau-dān, s.m. The gift of a cow; an offering of a cow
s.m. A cow's tooth; — yellow orpiment; — a white fossil substance (apparently an earthy salt)
s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds
s.f. 'Dust of the earth'; sunset (the time at which mist seems to rise from the earth); evening, twilight
s.m. Cow's milk; buttermilk; curdled or coagulated milk
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī). Reared on cow's milk; one who is reared on cow's milk, a child; — a sodomite
s.f. A vessel for holding milk, &c.; a milk-pail

s.m., or go-roćanā, s.f. A bright yellow pigment prepared from the urine or the bile of a cow, or vomited in the shape of scibulœ by that animal (it is employed in painting and dyeing, and is of especial use in marking the foreheads of Hindūs with the tilak or sectarial mark; it is also used in medicine as a sedative, tonic, and anthelmintic remedy, &c.)
or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

(gosh = gos, gen. of go), s.m. A cow's foot or hoof; — mark or impression of a cow's foot; — puddle or water sufficient to fill a cow's foot-mark; a small puddle; — a measure of capacity (as much as a cow's foot-print would hold)
vulg. goshṭh, s.m. An abode for cattle; cow-house, cow-pen; — any place where animals are kept; — a station of cowherds
s.f. An assembly, a meeting; a society, an association; a confederacy; — family connexions (esp. the dependant or junior branches); — conversation, discourse, dialogue; dispute

s.m. A cow's ear; — a species of deer, Antilope picta (syn. nīl-gāʼo); — a mule; — a kind of serpent; — a span (from the tip of the thumb to that of the ring-finger); — a place of pilgrimage on the Malabar coast (sacred to Śiva); Śiva (as there worshipped)
(for go-karṇī), s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides, and its flower
s.m. The plant Asteracantha longifolia, and Ruellia longifolia, and Tribulus languinosus
s.m. A herd of kine; a multitude of cattle; a cow-station, cow-house; — a village or tract of the Jamna (the country around Vr̤indāvan, the residence of Krishṇ during his youth)
s.m. A measure of distance (being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard)
s.f. The side of a river, or a tank, &c. at which cows drink
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), Noxious to kine; — he who or that which kills kine; one who has killed a cow
s.m. 'Cow-heaven'; the heaven of Vishṇu, or of Krishṇa

vulg. gomtī, s.f. Name of a river in Northern India (it falls into the Ganges between Banāras and Gāzīpūr); — name of a Vedic hymn (a prayer or formula to be repeated during expiation for killing a cow); — cow-dung
or gau-mukh, s.m. A kind of musical instrument (a sort of horn or trumpet); — a hole in a wall (of a peculiar shape), made by thieves, &c.; a breach (syn. seṅdh)

or gau-mukhī, s.f. The chasm in the Himālaya mountains through which the Ganges flows (erroneously conceived to be shaped like a cow's mouth); — a cloth bag containing a rosary (the beads of which are counted by the hand thrust inside)
or gau-mutr, s.m. Cow's urine (which has to be imbibed in certain kinds of penance)

or go-medak, s.m. A gem or precious stone brought from the Himālaya and the Indus (described as of four sorts, — white, pale yellow, red, and dark blue); a kind of yellowish or tawny gem, a topaz; — go-meda-sannibha, s.m. Chalcedony or opal
s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow
s.m. (lit. 'having a nose like that of an ox'), A large kind of serpent, a boa-constrictor

s.m. lit. 'Nourisher of kine'; name of a celebrated hill in Vrindāvan, about fifteen miles to the west of Mathurā (it is the Parnassus of the Hindūs; and is said to have been lifted up and supported by Krishṇa upon one finger for seven days, to shelter the cowherds from a storm of rain sent by Indra to test Krishṇa's divinity; — hence Krishṇ is called Govardhan-dhar, and Giri-dhar, 'the hill-supporter')
s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

def: s. f. A vessel for holding milk, &c., a milkpail.
n. m. Sandy soil.
s.m. Fields near a village; homestead.
n. m. The homestead; fields near the village.
s.f. = goṛhā.

ety: goṛh˚ = goṛ˚ = koṛ = Prk. कोडे(इ) or कोड(इ) = S. कोटय(ते), rt. कुट्
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch; to dig, to excavate; to hollow out, scoop out.
v. n. S. गुहा a pit. To excavate; hollow; scoop out.
s.f. A kind of porcelain (which breaks the moment poison is put into it); — a plate.
more: s. f. A kind of porcelain, which breaks the moment poison is put into it.
